SEVERAL ROADWAYS WERE IMPASSABLE DUE TO HIGH WATER IN DOWNTOWN MARION. SEVERAL ROADS WERE CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER. FIRE DEPT CONDUCTED SEVERAL WATER RESCUES OF PEOPLE IN CARS STRANDED BY HIGH WATER ON ROADS. SEVERAL LOCAL BUSINESSES SAID WATER WAS TWO FEET DEEP OUTSIDE THEIR BUSINESS AND OVER THE CURB AND SIDEWALKS INTO PARKING LOTS.

SEVERAL LOCAL BUSINESSES SAID WATER WAS TWO FEET DEEP OUTSIDE THEIR BUSINESS. THE HIGH WATER WAS OVER THE CURBS ONTO THE SIDEWALKS AND PARKING LOTS.

SANDUSKY COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS DAMAGE TO A HOME AND GARAGE ON COUNTY ROAD 268, NORTH OF VICKERY. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

THIS WEAK TORNADO STARTED JUST ABOUT 300 FEET WEST OF N VICKERY ROAD AND 1200 FEET NORTH OF WHITMORE ROAD. THIS TORNADO MOVED GENERALLY FROM WEST SOUTHWEST TO EAST NORTHEAST. THE FIELD WEST OF THE VICKERY ROAD REVEALED A PATH SHOWING A TRACK WITH SPIRALS. THE TORNADO DAMAGED A HOME WITH SHINGLES AND PARTS OF FLAT WOOD BLOWN OFF, WOODEN DECK MOVED SEVERAL FEET, DETACHED GARAGE MOVED FROM FOUNDATION, AND SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED. DEBRIS WAS SCATTERED ABOUT 1200 FEET EAST NORTHEAST OF THE DAMAGED HOME INTO THE OPEN FIELD. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR WAS CONFIRMED BY HOMEOWNER.
MESONET STATION XSDB 2 NNE SANDUSKY.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-1 TORNADO IN HINCKLEY TOWNSHIP. TORNADO BEGAN NEAR TURNSTONE COURT AND MOVED EAST BEFORE ENDING IN HINCKLEY RESERVATION.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-0 EAST OF SEVILLE AND WEST OF WADSWORTH IN MEDINA COUNTY. THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF RAWIGA ROAD AND TRACKED NORTHEAST INTO A CORN FIELD LEAVING A TRACK WITH SPIRALS BEFORE LIFTING. THE TORNADO WAS 50 YARDS WIDE AND PATH WAS 0.3 MILE.
NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-1 TORNADO IN WICKLIFFE. TORNADO BEGAN ON BRIAR COURT AND CONTINUED EAST TOWARD PINE RIDGE COUNTRY CLUB BEFORE DISSIPATING.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-0 TORNADO IN HUDSON. A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF STATE ROUTE 91 AND MIDDLETON ROAD AND MOVED NORTHEAST FOR A BRIEF TIME.
NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-1 TORNADO IN JACKSON TOWNSHIP IN STARK COUNTY. THE TORNADO BEGAN NEAR NORTH PARK AND CONTINUED EAST TO LAKE CABLE BEFORE ENDING WEST OF I-77 NEAR STARK STATE COLLEGE.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMS AN EF-0 NEAR AURORA. THE TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN WEST OF A LARGE 4-STORY BUILDING NORTHWEST OF LENA DRIVE AND PAGE ROAD. THE TORNADO MOVED EAST OVER THE PARKING LOT. ROOF AC UNIT WAS BLOW OVER, ROOF DAMAGE, AND TREE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED AND SCATTERED ACROSS THE PARKING LOT.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-1 TORNADO IN MECCA IN TRUMBULL COUNTY. THE TORNADO BEGAN EAST OF MOSQUITO CREEK LAKE AND MOVED THROUGH GRIFFITH AND EDGEWATER DRIVES PRODUCING DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES AND NUMEROUS TREES. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST EAST OF PHILLIPS RICE ROAD.
